Name: curvature-aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Training-Free Optimization via Geometric Subspace Diagnostics
Short Hypothesis: The curvature of the loss landscape in low-dimensional subspaces governs the sensitivity of model activations to precision degradation, enabling a training-free precision routing mechanism based on geometric diagnostics.
Related Work: Existing precision optimization methods rely on static profiling or heuristic calibration, often failing to adapt to dynamic input distributions. Recent work has explored spectral quantization and subspace projection for efficiency, but none have explicitly linked curvature diagnostics to precision sensitivity. Our proposal uniquely integrates curvature-aware geometric analysis with deterministic precision routing, providing a mathematically transparent and resource-efficient alternative.
Abstract: Mixed-precision inference promises significant gains in latency and memory efficiency, but current methods often rely on costly calibration or static profiling, failing to adapt to dynamic input distributions. We propose Curvature-Aware Precision Routing (CAPR), a training-free framework that dynamically allocates numerical precision based on the curvature of the loss landscape in low-dimensional subspaces. CAPR computes geometric diagnostics—such as Hessian conditioning and gradient covariance decay—on-the-fly using randomized SVD, mapping them to a precision-switching policy that enforces strict latency budgets. We theoretically ground the routing mechanism with PAC-Bayes bounds that link curvature to generalization stability under quantization noise. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks, CAPR achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines. By replacing qualitative fragility claims with formalized risk bounds, CAPR provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference under natural distribution shifts.

Experiments.Description: Evaluate CAPR on WILDS benchmarks under controlled distribution shifts.
Experiments.Method: Compare CAPR against static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines, measuring throughput, accuracy, and latency under varying input distributions.

Experiments.Description: Validate geometric diagnostics using randomized SVD.
Experiments.Method: Compute condition number and spectral decay rate of hidden states on-the-fly, mapping them to precision-switching policies.
Derive PAC-Bayes bounds linking curvature to generalization stability under quantization noise, validating empirically on single-GPU setups.

Risk Factors and Limitations: The reliance on randomized SVD may introduce computational overhead in high-dimensional settings.
The effectiveness of curvature diagnostics may degrade under extreme distribution shifts.
The proposed method assumes access to low-dimensional subspaces, which may not always be available in complex models.
